questions.question: Which is appropriate for a formal email to your employer?
options: Hey boss! Can't come tomorrow. Sick 😷
Hi, I won't be able to make it tomorrow.
Dear Mr. Brown, I am writing to inform you that I am unable to attend work tomorrow due to illness.
Mr. Brown, I'm sick so I can't come.
questions.explanation: C uses formal greeting, no contractions, polite formal language.

questions.question: Which sign-off is correct for a formal letter to someone you don't know?
questions.explanation: 'Yours faithfully' is used when you don't know the person's name.

questions.question: Which is the formal version of 'find out'?
Which opening is correct for a formal email?
options: I hope you're well! I wanna ask...
I am writing to inquire about...
So basically, I need help with...
questions.explanation: B uses formal language and states purpose clearly. No contractions.

questions.question: Convert to formal: 'The results were really good.'
Which subject line is best for a formal email about a job application?
options: Application for Marketing Manager Position - Ref: MKT2025
questions.explanation: C is specific, professional, and includes a reference number.

questions.question: Which sentence is written in appropriate formal style?
options: I can't make it to the meeting.
I will not be able to attend the meeting.
Meeting's not gonna happen for me.
Won't be at the meeting, sorry!
questions.explanation: B has no contractions, uses full words, professional tone.

questions.question: In a formal letter, where does YOUR address go?
questions.explanation: Your address goes top right. Recipient's address goes left (below date).

questions.question: Which situation requires INFORMAL writing?
options: A complaint letter to a company
A text message to your best friend
questions.explanation: Informal writing is appropriate for personal communication with friends/family.

questions.question: Fix this formal email opening: 'Hey Dr. Smith! I'm a student and I wanna ask about the assignment. Can you help ASAP? Thanks!'
options: Dear Dr. Smith, I'm writing to ask about the assignment.
Dear Dr. Smith, I am writing to inquire about the assignment details. I would appreciate your guidance at your earliest convenience.
Hi Dr. Smith, Could you please tell me about the assignment?
Dear Dr. Smith, I need help with the assignment immediately.
questions.explanation: B has: formal greeting, no contractions, formal vocabulary 'inquire', polite closing phrase, no slang/abbreviations.
